key_points: Blount Hall repeatedly called out as the worst dorm option
One student described their room as looking like a jail cell
300 new beds added after record enrollment — existing stock is strained
key_quotes: It looks like I was in a jail cell
So glad I never had to live in Blount
as a freshman this year, this video was rlly helpful AND u tellin the whole truth ab blount it's crazy

key_points: Greek life dominates campus culture — non-D9 students can feel left out
Making friends described as cliquey and difficult at first
Homecoming and yard culture are genuinely beloved highlights
key_quotes: people be really weird when it comes to making new friends lol
I don't like how much HBCUs center fraternities and sororities
Greek elitism rules most of our campuses

key_points: Almost no direct student comments about dining quality
One student implied food scarcity: 'a few nights I had sleep for dinner'
No visible praise for dining options in any source
key_quotes: a few nights I had sleep for dinner
They always cuttin the grass, that's what you can count on

key_points: Financial stress and food insecurity mentioned as real burdens for students
A student at a nearby HBCU died from targeted harassment — homophobia and exclusion cited
No dedicated mental health support resources mentioned by any student
key_quotes: a few nights I had sleep for dinner
Everybody is Jack and Jill, entire family D9… makes me not want to be here
He went to my school. He did a lot of great things through his leadership.

key_points: MacKenzie Scott donated $63M to Morgan — major endowment boost underway
Students warn debt load is often not worth starting salary outcomes
Alumni regret debt levels; one said 'if I could do it again financially, I would'
key_quotes: Address academic costs, room and board costs and make education truly AFFORDABLE
$100,000+ in debt to start off at $50,000 a year with limited upward mobility
I love my HBCU, but if I could do it again in a different way financially, I would without question

key_points: Nursing program hit 100% NCLEX pass rate — #1 in Maryland, beats Johns Hopkins
Academic rigor is real; students reference sleep deprivation managing workload
Medical school reportedly in the works under President Wilson
key_quotes: Higher than Johns Hopkins University. Congratulations to the National Treasure that is Morgan State University.
Wait 'til Morgan State opens up its new medical school. That place will be unstoppable.
We get more done with less, IYKYK.

key_points: President Wilson praised widely — seen as visionary and results-driven
Record enrollment triggered reactive housing expansion, not proactive planning
No major scandals flagged; administration appears functional and donor-trusted
key_quotes: MacKenzie Scott's renewed investment in Morgan is a resounding testament to the work we've done
Dr. Wilson ain't playing around
Morgan may have more students than NCAT soon. This housing is needed.

key_points: Campus sits in a high-crime area of Baltimore — students are aware and concerned
A shooting occurred on or near campus involving a freshman student's dorm
Campus maintenance (grass cutting) is reliable but surroundings draw consistent warnings
key_quotes: yeah the area morgan is in isn't the best
Most HBCUs are in disadvantage areas… Morgan should educate students on HBCUs
This was one of the most helpful college videos I've seen cuz I wasn't even thinking about if the place around the campus had a high crime rate

key_points: Nursing grads have near-guaranteed licensure with 100% NCLEX pass rate
Engineering and general graduates cite frustrating job market and employer indifference to degrees
HBCU network and alumni connections seen as long-term career asset
key_quotes: Noone told me how little job opportunities there would be and how little employers care about qualifications. Its who you know not what you know.
Morgan State University Graduate Launches AI Agent For Overlooked HBCU And Small-School NIL Athletes

key_points: Large philanthropic donations (MacKenzie Scott $63M) are improving institutional resources
Students still carry significant debt relative to post-grad earning potential
Nursing ROI is exceptional; other programs more uncertain
key_quotes: $100,000+ in debt to start off at $50,000 a year with limited upward mobility
I love my HBCU, but if I could do it again in a different way financially, I would without question

key_points: Strong institutional pride and community identity — 'Bear Nation' is real
Students warn incoming freshmen to be socially prepared for cliques and adjustment
Campus is improving fast; students who stuck it out are glad they did
key_quotes: By the end of my second semester, I realized it was exactly where I wanted to be. SO glad I didn't transfer.
